name: Regular Unleaded
description: Regular unleaded gasoline (87 octane) is the most common fuel type used by the majority of passenger vehicles. It is the least expensive grade available at most gas stations.
vehicles: Most standard passenger cars and trucks

name: Midgrade
description: Midgrade gasoline (89 octane) sits between regular and premium. Some older vehicles or those with slightly higher compression ratios may benefit from this grade.
vehicles: Some older vehicles and certain performance cars

name: Premium
description: Premium gasoline (91-93 octane) is required or recommended for high-performance and luxury vehicles with turbocharged or high-compression engines.
vehicles: Luxury cars, sports cars, turbocharged engines

name: Diesel
description: Diesel fuel powers compression-ignition engines found in trucks, SUVs, and commercial vehicles. Diesel engines are typically more fuel-efficient than gasoline engines.
vehicles: Diesel trucks, SUVs, and commercial vehicles

name: E85 Ethanol
description: E85 is a blend of 85% ethanol and 15% gasoline. It has a higher octane rating than regular gasoline and burns cleaner, but offers lower fuel economy.
